data.title: Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) Margin Strength And 43.9% EPS Growth Test Slower Growth Narrative
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) just opened Q1 2026 earnings season with a solid set of headline figures, anchored by Q4 2025 revenue of US$1.2b and basic EPS of US$2.98, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$4.7b and EPS of US$10.46 that reflect its latest run rate. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from US$1.11b in Q4 2024 to US$1.20b in Q4 2025, while quarterly EPS shifted from US$1.87 to US$2.98. This sets up a story where higher earnings and a 23.2% trailing net margin give investors a results season focused on profitability quality and staying power.

See our full analysis for Cboe Global Markets.

With the raw numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the key narratives around Cboe’s growth, risks, and overall profitability story.

See what the community is saying about Cboe Global Markets

Advertisement

Margins Hold At 23.2% While Growth Slows

  • Cboe’s trailing net profit margin sits at 23.2%, compared with 18.6% a year earlier, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$4.7b and net income of about US$1.1b.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that recent 43.9% earnings growth and a five year earnings growth rate of 23.3% per year sit next to forecast revenue and earnings growth of only about 4.2% per year. This heavily supports the idea that past expansion in derivatives and data is already visible in the numbers, but also tests how much further margin gains can realistically go if top line growth slows to those forecast levels.
    • Bulls point to high margin, recurring data and index licensing revenue as a driver of earnings quality, and the current 23.2% net margin against last year’s 18.6% fits that story of stronger profitability.
    • At the same time, forecasts of around 4.2% annual growth in both revenue and earnings are much lower than the 43.9% earnings growth just reported over the past year, which challenges the idea that the recent pace can be extrapolated.
Over the last year Cboe’s expanding margins and earnings growth have made the bullish case look very compelling. If that is the view you lean toward it is worth seeing how supporters connect these numbers to future growth in more detail in the 🐂 Cboe Global Markets Bull Case.

P/E At 31.3x With DCF Fair Value Below Price

  • The shares trade on a 31.3x P/E at a price of US$326.96, compared with a US Capital Markets industry average of 42.4x and a peer average of 29.7x, while the DCF fair value cited is US$282.39.
  • Bears highlight that the share price is above the DCF fair value and only modestly below the 31.3x P/E implied by the 303.86 analyst price target. This fits a cautious view that a lot of the recent 43.9% earnings growth and 23.2% margin is already reflected in the valuation and that slower forecast growth of about 4.2% per year could limit upside from here.
    • The gap between the current US$326.96 share price and the US$282.39 DCF fair value provides a concrete reference point for those arguing the shares screen as expensive on that metric.
    • With the analyst price target of 303.86 sitting below today’s price and forecasts calling for mid single digit growth, skeptics can reasonably question how much room is left if growth normalises to those levels.
Skeptical investors often focus on the spread between price, fair value estimates, and slower forecast growth. If that sounds like your concern it is worth seeing how that case is laid out in full in the 🐻 Cboe Global Markets Bear Case.

LTM EPS Of US$10.46 Underpins Mixed Growth Story

  • On a trailing basis, EPS totals US$10.46 with trailing twelve month revenue of US$4.7b, compared with trailing revenue of US$4.1b and net income of US$761m a year ago.
  • Consensus narrative points to broad based growth in derivatives, data, and global trading, and the trailing numbers back that up on profitability. Yet the combination of 23.2% net margin, 43.9% earnings growth, and forecasts of roughly 4.2% annual revenue and earnings growth gives you a mixed picture where the recent past looks strong but the forward pace embedded in models is much more measured.
    • The move from US$4.1b to US$4.7b in trailing revenue and from US$761m to about US$1.1b in trailing net income lines up with the view that data and international segments have been important earnings contributors.
    • However, analysts expecting earnings to reach about US$1.4b by around 2029 with profit margins rising to 46.3% are assuming a very different phase compared with the last 12 months, so it helps to think about whether that shift from high growth to slower, higher margin expansion matches your own expectations.

Mixed signals on growth and valuation can be confusing, so it helps to review the numbers, weigh both sides, and decide what fits your plan. To see how the current story balances optimism with concern, take a close look at the 3 key rewards and 1 important warning sign.

data.title: Ford Employee Pricing Push Tests Balance Between Volume And Margins
data.description:
• Ford Motor (NYSE:F) has launched a nationwide employee pricing program for all customers on new 2025 and 2026 model year vehicles.
  • The campaign, branded "American Value. For American Values.", runs through July 6 and is tied to the upcoming US Semiquincentennial.
  • The offer extends a broad pricing incentive that is usually limited to employees, aiming to respond to consumer affordability pressures.

For you as an investor, this highlights how Ford is positioning its core US business at a time when many buyers are focused on monthly payments and overall ownership costs. NYSE:F remains heavily exposed to the traditional truck, SUV and car segments, while also investing in newer technologies and drivetrains. Large, company wide pricing programs like this can influence sales mix between models and trims, as well as dealer inventories.

Looking ahead, the key questions are how this campaign shapes unit volumes, profit per vehicle and brand perception once the program ends on July 6. You may want to watch for any follow up comments from Ford on customer response, dealer feedback and whether similar pricing actions appear again in future promotions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Ford Motor by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Ford Motor.

📰 Beyond the headline: 2 risks and 3 things going right for Ford Motor that every investor should see.

For Ford, extending employee pricing to most 2025 and 2026 Ford and Lincoln models looks like a volume and brand move that sits alongside a strong Q1 and higher 2026 guidance. After reporting Q1 revenue of US$43.3b and net income of US$2.5b, management is putting price in focus at a time when affordability is front and center for buyers. The offer simplifies pricing compared with the usual rebate and negotiation model, which can help Ford defend share against General Motors and Stellantis, especially in full size trucks and SUVs where monthly payments often drive decisions. The campaign also lines up with Ford’s comments about offering more entry level trims, which can support showroom traffic while Ford works on software and services growth in Ford Pro and newer vehicle platforms.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Ford Motor Narrative

  • The program supports the narrative theme that Ford is using cost discipline and portfolio choices to protect margins while keeping volumes competitive in core trucks, SUVs and commercial vehicles.
  • A broad pricing incentive can challenge the margin improvement story if higher unit volumes do not offset lower average prices or if mix shifts away from higher margin trims.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on software and EV platforms. A nationwide discount campaign tied to ICE and hybrid heavy line ups may not be fully reflected in assumptions about how Ford balances price, volume and earnings quality.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Ford Motor to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that debt is not well covered by operating cash flow, so aggressive pricing that pressures margins could make balance sheet strength more important to track.
  • ⚠️ The dividend yield of about 5.05% is not well covered by earnings, so any sustained pressure on profitability from promotional activity can increase questions about payout flexibility.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 90.09% per year, so if this campaign supports unit volumes and keeps Ford’s product base relevant with buyers, it could help management work toward those expectations.
  • 🎁 Ford is trading at 3.9% below one estimate of fair value and at what is described as good value versus peers and the wider auto industry, which may give some valuation support while the pricing push runs.

What To Watch Going Forward

After this promotion, focus on how Ford describes the trade off between volume and profit per vehicle, especially in Ford Blue and Ford Pro. Watch for commentary on whether customers shifted toward lower priced trims, how dealers managed inventory, and whether similar pricing appears in later quarters. It can also be useful to see how this program interacts with Ford’s ongoing EV reset and software centric plans, including whether it helps keep ICE and hybrid volumes steady while next generation platforms are developed.

To stay up to date on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Ford Motor, head to the community page for Ford Motor to follow the top community narratives.

  • This is the first high-ABV RTD offering tied to the top-selling Modelo brand in Constellation’s portfolio.

For investors tracking NYSE:STZ, this launch sits at the intersection of two important themes: premium Mexican beer and the expanding RTD alcoholic beverage category. Constellation Brands is using the strength of Modelo to participate more directly in demand for higher-proof, flavored cans that fit casual and social drinking occasions.

For now, the key question for readers is how a high-ABV, flavor-focused line like Modelo Chelada Suprema fits into broader brand building and RTD category competition. Future company updates may give more clarity on distribution reach, consumer adoption and how this product family fits alongside other beer and RTD offerings in the portfolio.

Modelo Chelada Suprema gives Constellation Brands another way to monetize Modelo’s position as the No. 1 beer in the U.S. by dollar sales, this time in the higher strength ready-to-drink segment. By focusing on 8% ABV, 24 ounce cans and fruit flavors such as Mangonada and Tropical, Constellation is leaning into demand from Gen Z and Millennial drinkers who are open to new formats and international flavor profiles. That helps Constellation compete more directly with RTD offerings from players like Anheuser Busch InBev and Molson Coors, while staying close to the Mexican beer identity that has worked for the core Modelo franchise.

How This Fits Into The Constellation Brands Narrative

  • The launch supports the existing narrative that disciplined product development in beer can help Constellation maintain strong margins and make better use of the brewery investments already planned in Mexico.
  • If higher input costs or future tariffs increase the expense of producing 24 ounce, high-ABV cans, this RTD expansion could pressure the margin resilience that the narrative expects from the beer segment.
  • The narrative focuses on broad beer growth and margin targets, and may not fully reflect how consumer interest in higher strength RTD products and international flavors could influence mix, pricing, and marketing spend.

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 1 key risk, and a higher strength RTD line could face greater regulatory or taxation sensitivity if rules around alcohol content shift.
  • ⚠️ Concentrating new RTD growth behind a single brand may increase reliance on Modelo’s appeal if consumer preferences change away from high-ABV canned products.
  • 🎁 The new line targets flavor seeking Gen Z and Millennial drinkers, which may help sustain consumer loyalty in beer at a time when some categories face slower volume growth.
  • 🎁 If Chelada Suprema attracts incremental RTD spending rather than cannibalizing existing SKUs, it could support the positive rewards analysts see in revenue and earnings potential.

From here, pay attention to how distributors and retailers position Modelo Chelada Suprema on shelves and in coolers, and whether Constellation expands beyond the initial two flavors or pack formats. data.title: South32 Resets Hermosa Taylor With Higher Costs And Longer Mine Life
data.description:
• South32 (ASX:S32) has flagged about US$1.1b in additional capital expenditure for the Taylor project at its Hermosa asset, taking total projected spend to roughly US$3.3b.
  • First production has been pushed back from mid 2027 to mid 2028, with full capacity now targeted for FY2031, one year later than previously indicated.
  • The update includes a 52% uplift in Ore Reserves, an extended mine life to around 33 years, and extra decline infrastructure aimed at improving long term operating flexibility.

For investors watching ASX:S32 at around A$4.12, this Hermosa Taylor update combines higher upfront costs and timing delays with a materially larger ore base and much longer potential mine life. The share price is up 16.1% year to date and 49.6% over the past year, even after a 4.6% decline over the past week and an 8.0% decline over the past month, so this project reset lands after a strong run in the stock.

From here, attention is likely to focus on how South32 manages execution risk at Taylor, including contractor performance and cost control, in the context of a longer life asset. For readers, the key questions are whether the enlarged resource, 33 year mine life and planned infrastructure changes provide enough long term value to justify the higher US$3.3b capital bill and the shift in the production timeline.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for South32 by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on South32.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 3 things going right for South32 that every investor should see.

This update effectively reframes Hermosa Taylor as a larger, more complex long life growth project rather than a quick new source of cash flow. The US$1.1b uplift in growth capital to about US$3.3b, together with a one year delay to first production and to nameplate capacity, increases execution risk and ties up more capital for longer. At the same time, the 52% Ore Reserve uplift, 17% higher life of mine production and roughly 33 year mine life extend South32’s exposure to zinc, lead and silver, with Peake and Clark adding copper and flexibility options over time. For readers, the trade off is clear: a bigger multi decade ore system and integrated underground plan versus higher upfront spend, contractor performance issues and revised operating cost assumptions at roughly US$100 per tonne.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The South32 Narrative

  • The Hermosa update aligns with the existing focus on building future facing base metals exposure through Taylor, Sierra Gorda and related copper options, reinforcing South32’s shift away from coal and toward longer life zinc, lead, silver and potential copper production.
  • The increased capital budget, shaft construction challenges and later ramp up to FY2031 highlight exactly the project execution and cost inflation risks that the narrative already flags as potential pressure points for future cash flow and returns.
  • The specific integration of Clark decline access, extra ore handling capacity and Peake’s 32% larger copper resource, along with the updated US$3.1b to US$4.5b NPV range, may not be fully reflected in existing storyline assumptions about Hermosa’s optionality.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for South32 to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Higher growth capital of about US$3.3b, concentrated between Q4 FY2026 and H2 FY2028, raises funding and cost overrun risk if inflation, US tariffs or input prices move further or if additional scope changes emerge.
  • ⚠️ Contractor underperformance on shaft construction and the need for strengthened oversight underline execution risk, which could affect timing, unit costs and ultimately project returns if productivity does not stabilise.
  • 🎁 A 52% increase in Ore Reserves to 99 Mt, a roughly 33 year initial operating life and 17% higher life of mine production support Hermosa Taylor’s role as a long duration, low cost producer of zinc, lead and silver.
  • 🎁 The Peake copper resource uplift to 33 Mt and the Clark decline link offer additional sources of future copper production and mine life flexibility that could differentiate South32 versus diversified peers with US growth options such as BHP or Rio Tinto.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the key watchpoints are whether South32 can improve shaft sinking productivity under the revised plan, hold operating unit costs near the US$100 per tonne assumption and deliver the more gradual ramp up to FY2031 without further delays. Investors may also want to track how much of the roughly US$2.1b remaining growth capital through H2 FY2028 is committed to contracted pricing, any further updates on Peake’s potential copper link into Taylor and whether additional surface infrastructure de bottlenecking actually supports production above current design rates.
